---
title: "Quest 10: Number Game - Guess and Check"
subtitle: "Build a Simple Interactive Rust Program"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🎲 QUEST 10 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 10 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [Open Notebook](../jupyterlite/content/10-number-game.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/10-number-game.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Small games are great practice for conditionals and loops.
Small improvements each run add up quickly.

## 💡 Core Game Logic

```rust
fn main() {
    let secret = 7;
    let guess = 5;

    if guess == secret {
        println!("Correct!");
    } else if guess < secret {
        println!("Too low");
    } else {
        println!("Too high");
    }
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Track how many guesses were used and stop after 3 attempts.
If that works, try one new secret number and test again.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn main() {
    let secret = 7;
    let guesses = vec![4, 7, 9];

    for (turn, guess) in guesses.iter().enumerate() {
        if *guess == secret {
            println!("Turn {}: correct!", turn + 1);
            break;
        } else if *guess < secret {
            println!("Turn {}: too low", turn + 1);
        } else {
            println!("Turn {}: too high", turn + 1);
        }
    }
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 10 Complete
You built a tiny game loop using Rust control flow.
Next: [Quest 11: Recursion](11-recursion.qmd)
:::
